narrative.headline: Transmission and drivetrain complaints lead the 2020 Ford Edge
narrative.summary: Of 283 owner complaints filed with NHTSA, the power train (transmission and drivetrain) is by far the most reported area, with 160 reports (57%). The engine is next at 41 reports (14%), followed by the electrical system at 22 (8%). Safety incidents are spread thinly across the top issues: the power train shows 1 fire, 2 crashes, and 3 injuries, while forward collision avoidance has the most crashes (3) and injuries (3) relative to its size. Focus a pre-purchase inspection on the transmission and drivetrain first.

issues.sample: ENGINE CUT OFF WHILE DRIVING AT HIGHWAY SPEED IN HEAVY DALLAS TRAFFIC.  REQUIRED COMING TO COMPLETE STOP TO RESTART VEHICLE AFTER CROSSING THREE LANES TO REACH SHOULDER OF INTERSTATE.  DANGEROUS.  DAYLIGHT, MID DAY, CLEAR WEATHER CONDITIONS, HAD BEEN DRIVING 1.5 HOURS; MILEAGE ON
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The single largest complaint area: 160 reports (57% of all complaints), including 1 fire, 2 crashes, and 3 injuries. Have the transmission and drivetrain inspected and test the car thoroughly before buying.

issues.sample: ENGINE CUT OFF WHILE DRIVING AT HIGHWAY SPEED IN HEAVY DALLAS TRAFFIC.  REQUIRED COMING TO COMPLETE STOP TO RESTART VEHICLE AFTER CROSSING THREE LANES TO REACH SHOULDER OF INTERSTATE.  DANGEROUS.  DAYLIGHT, MID DAY, CLEAR WEATHER CONDITIONS, HAD BEEN DRIVING 1.5 HOURS; MILEAGE ON
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The second most reported area: 41 reports (14%), with 1 fire and 1 injury among them. Check the engine carefully and watch for stalling on a test drive.

issues.sample: REAR VIEW CAMERA FAILED ON MANY OCCASIONS WHILE DRIVING IN REVERSE IN SHOPPING STORE PARKING LOTS AND WHILE DRIVING IN REVERSE OUT OF DRIVEWAYS INTO PUBLIC STREETS. ON A FEW OCCASIONS REAR VIEW CAMERA FAILED TO DISPLAY MOTOR VEHICLES AND PEDESTRIANS. REPORTED ISSUE TO FORD DEALER
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 22 reports (8%), with 1 crash and 2 injuries. Verify the electrical system, including the rear-view camera, works reliably.

issues.sample: RECENTLY, MY 2020 FORD EDGE, WITH 1,800 MILES, DISPLAYED A WARNING THAT THE PRE-COLLISION ASSIST WAS NOT AVAILABLE.  AFTER COMPLETING THE POSSIBLE SOLUTIONS INDICATED BY THE OWNER'S MANUAL WITHOUT SUCCESS, I TOOK THE EDGE INTO A FORD DEALERSHIP. THE SERVICE TECH'S ATTEMPT TO REPR
issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Fewer reports at 17 (6%), but the most safety-flagged for its size, with 3 crashes and 3 injuries. Confirm the pre-collision assist and related driver-assist features are working.

issues.sample: VEHICLE transmission while in park failed to remain and moved backward on its own. My safety was at risk as a tractor trailer pulled into rest stop and had to brake b4 colliding with the said vehicle, also if anything was behind me it could have been at risk. unknown unknown NO t
issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: The smallest of the top issues at 12 reports (4%), with 1 crash and 1 injury. Still worth checking the backup safety systems before buying.
